benefits.title: Programmes can tell confident, nuanced graduate futures stories
benefits.statement: Programme teams have clearer evidence and language for explaining what their programme makes possible for students and where that value shows up in graduate futures.
benefits.beneficiary: Programme teams, departments, students, applicants, recruitment teams and faculties
benefits.successLooksLike: Programme teams can make confident, nuanced and evidence-informed claims about their excellence, distinctive strengths and students' graduate futures, without reducing that story to a single metric.
evidenceOfSuccess: Programme reflections, profiles or story materials explain what programmes enable for students using evidence, context and academic judgement.
Programme teams recognise themselves in the story and feel able to use it with students, applicants, colleagues or external audiences.
Student-facing or applicant-facing claims are evidence-linked, context-sensitive and agreed with the relevant programme teams.

enabledBy.outputTitle: First working graduate futures evidence pack

benefits.title: Programmes identify where to strengthen practice
benefits.statement: Programme and department teams use graduate futures evidence to identify where curriculum, skills, opportunity, support or articulation work could make their programme story stronger.
benefits.beneficiary: Programme teams, departments, faculties, Careers and Employability Service and students
benefits.successLooksLike: Teams can distinguish strengths to claim, questions to investigate and focused areas for development, without treating the evidence as a deficit report or judgement on programme quality.
evidenceOfSuccess: Guided conversations capture agreed questions, practical local actions or areas for further support.
Actions or focus areas are taken into normal programme, department or faculty planning routes rather than remaining as standalone project paperwork.
Programme teams can describe how the evidence helped them see both existing strengths and where more is possible for students.

enabledBy.outputTitle: Examples of programme insight and story

benefits.title: Evidence is used responsibly and credibly
benefits.statement: Colleagues understand the caveats, context and limits of the evidence and use it alongside academic judgement rather than as a blunt performance signal.
benefits.beneficiary: Programme teams, departments, faculties, students and senior education leaders
benefits.successLooksLike: Programme-facing use of graduate futures evidence is fair, interpretable and developmental, avoiding ranking, league-table or punitive readings.
evidenceOfSuccess: Feedback from academic and professional services colleagues suggests the packs are useful, fair and interpretable when caveats and context are visible.
Programme conversations use academic judgement to challenge over-simple readings of destinations, outcomes, aspiration data or comparator evidence.
Pack, profile, story and student-facing materials include appropriate caveats and avoid unsupported or over-claimed conclusions.

benefits.title: King's gains a clearer institutional view of graduate futures
benefits.statement: Shared strengths, questions, student pathways and support needs can be seen across programmes while preserving disciplinary nuance, local context and academic judgement.
benefits.beneficiary: King's Edge, faculties, senior education leaders, Careers and Employability Service, programme teams and students
benefits.successLooksLike: King's can see patterns across programme stories and graduate futures evidence without flattening difference or turning the synthesis into programme comparison.
evidenceOfSuccess: Faculty-level learning and institutional synthesis draw out strengths, recurring questions, student pathways and support needs across contexts.
Shared themes are discussed as learning, support and strategic development needs, not as programme league tables.
Institutional synthesis informs King's Edge work, faculty planning, graduate premium evidence, curriculum enhancement or skills-related activity.

enabledBy.outputTitle: Recommendations for related King's Edge work

benefits.title: Graduate futures evidence becomes part of normal review practice
benefits.statement: Graduate futures evidence, interpretation guidance and programme review become part of ongoing programme reflection and enhancement rather than a one-off mobilisation exercise.
benefits.beneficiary: Programme teams, departments, faculties, Careers and Employability Service, quality assurance and continual improvement owners and students
benefits.successLooksLike: The evidence packs and guided review process have a clear owner, refresh rhythm, access route and place in existing programme, department, faculty or institutional planning conversations.
evidenceOfSuccess: Ownership, refresh rhythm, access route and interpretation support for the evidence packs are confirmed.
Graduate futures evidence has an agreed place in quality assurance, continual improvement or programme enhancement cycles.
Ongoing feedback from colleagues who use the packs and review process leads to updates in evidence, guidance or support over time.

enabledBy.outputTitle: Quality assurance and continual improvement handoff

measures.title: Confidence and quality of programme graduate futures stories
measures.questionAnswered: Are programme teams able to tell confident, nuanced and evidence-informed stories about what their programmes enable?
measures.measure: Review of programme reflections, profile materials, story examples and programme-team feedback on whether the evidence helps them claim strengths and explain value with confidence.
measures.owner: Heads of Department, with support from Careers and Employability Service and Mark Peace
measures.cadence: During early programme partner review and annual synthesis
measures.baseline: Programme-level graduate futures stories are currently uneven and not consistently evidence-linked.
measures.target: Programme stories used in review, recruitment, open days or student-facing contexts are evidence-linked, context-sensitive and agreed with relevant programme teams.

measures.title: Quality of practice-strengthening actions
measures.questionAnswered: Does the process help programmes identify useful areas for strengthening practice without feeling judged or ranked?
measures.measure: Review of guided conversation outputs, agreed questions, practical local actions and evidence that actions are routed through normal programme, department or faculty planning.
measures.owner: Heads of Department and faculty education leads
measures.cadence: Termly during supported conversations and annual synthesis
measures.baseline: Actions from graduate futures evidence are not yet consistently captured or routed through local planning.
measures.target: Engaged programme groups produce proportionate reflections or actions that distinguish strengths, questions and areas where additional support or enhancement would be useful.

measures.title: Responsible evidence use and interpretation quality
measures.questionAnswered: Is graduate futures evidence being used fairly, credibly and with appropriate caveats?
measures.measure: Feedback and review of packs, guidance, programme conversations, profile materials and student-facing claims for responsible interpretation, visible caveats and avoidance of over-simple comparison.
measures.cadence: During testing, early programme partner review and annual synthesis
measures.baseline: Programme-level evidence use currently depends on local interpretation and may over-rely on headline indicators.
measures.target: Programme-facing materials and conversations use caveats, academic judgement and contextual interpretation, and avoid league-table or punitive readings.

measures.title: Institutional learning from programme graduate futures evidence
measures.questionAnswered: Is King's developing a clearer institutional view of graduate futures while preserving programme nuance?
measures.measure: Review of faculty-level learning, early institutional summary, institutional graduate futures synthesis and evidence that themes inform King's Edge, faculty planning, graduate premium evidence or related curriculum and skills activity.
measures.owner: Mark Peace, with Careers and Employability Service and faculty education leads
measures.cadence: Christmas 2026 early summary and annual synthesis during 2027/28
measures.baseline: King's does not yet have a coherent programme-level synthesis of graduate futures strengths, questions, support needs and student pathways.
measures.target: Institutional synthesis identifies strengths, recurring questions, support needs and implications for related work without reducing programmes to categories or rankings.

measures.title: Sustained use through normal review and enhancement cycles
measures.questionAnswered: Has graduate futures evidence become part of normal review and enhancement practice rather than a one-off project output?
measures.measure: Confirmation of evidence refresh rhythm, business-as-usual ownership, access route, interpretation support, quality assurance or continual improvement handoff and ongoing feedback process.
measures.owner: Careers and Employability Service, quality assurance and continual improvement owners and faculty education leads
measures.baseline: No agreed business-as-usual home, refresh rhythm or review route yet exists for programme-level graduate futures evidence.
measures.target: The data packs, interpretation guidance and review process have confirmed ownership and a recognised place in ongoing programme, department, faculty or institutional planning conversations.
